title: Human Development Index groups
subtitle: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.
caption: The 2023 world map of Human Development Index groups displays national performance across health, education, and living standards using a four-tier color scale: red for Low, yellow for Medium, light blue for High, and dark blue for Very high. The highest tier, Very high, covers most of North America — the United States and Canada — nearly all of Western and Northern Europe including the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Sweden, and Norway, as well as Australia, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, and several Gulf states such as the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia. The High group includes much of Latin America — Argentina, Chile, Uruguay, Brazil, Mexico — alongside Russia, China, Turkey, and Malaysia. Medium HDI nations appear in yellow across parts of Southeast Asia like Indonesia and the Philippines, North African countries including Egypt and Morocco, and scattered states in Southern Africa such as Botswana and Namibia. The Low category, shown in red, dominates sub-Saharan Africa — Nigeria, Ethiopia, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Sudan, Chad, Niger, Mali, Burkina Faso, Guinea, Sierra Leone, Liberia, Central African Republic, South Sudan, Mozambique, Madagascar — and also includes Yemen, Afghanistan, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Myanmar, Laos, Cambodia, and Haiti. Greenland and Somalia are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no available data. The geographic distribution shows a clear concentration of Very high HDI in temperate-zone industrialized economies, while Low HDI clusters heavily in tropical and conflict-affected regions of Africa and South Asia. The most visible pattern is the near-total absence of Very high HDI countries in sub-Saharan Africa and the overwhelming presence of Low HDI across that same region.
